titleStmt.title: Letter from Lily to Charlie Daly, 24 January 1923
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Lily

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Lily [ ], Dublin to Charles Daly, Drumboe Castle, Stranorlar, County Donegal, 24 January
1923. 'Fosey Meek' has been asking about him and has returned to his home. She has heard that Ballybunion Station 'has been wrecked'. 3 Pages and 1 Envelope.

correspAction.placeName: Drumboe Castle, Stranorlar, Tirconail, Donegal, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
I f not at this address please forward 24/1/23 Mr Charlie Daly.
Dromboe Castle,
Stranorlor,
Co. Donegal
P41/2/21/20 Dublin 24/1/23 A cara, I have been watching
out every day for a letter from
you. and hope you are not ill.
Every one of us here has had
a kind of flu Leo is still laid
up & I am just recovering.
In your letter to Lizzie you did not say if you received
the books. I hope you did & liked them. We do not know if Susie has returned yet as she has not called or sent any
his love. Let me know how you are getting along as soon as you have time. I have no Kerry news at all only I suppose you have heard that the Ballybunion Station has been wrecked. I will finish now with very best wishes from all here Your Sincere friend Lily _____ .
(2) word. I was speaking to a friend
of yours last week he told
me to tell you when writing
that Foxy Mick was asking
for you. and to say he has
gone to the home town. We are expecting Berny
to-night. He is coming
to bring Gerard. your prospective
brother-in-law back to school
to-morrow, and he is quite
sad at the thought of going.
He is here beside me & sends
